title: Configuration Management Service with OPA, etcd, and RAG Architecture
code:
graph TD subgraph "End Users" PLATFORM_ADMIN[Platform Admin] PROJECT_USER[Project User] end subgraph "Network Layer" LB["Load Balancer (HA Pair)"] end subgraph "Datacenter 1 (Active)" subgraph "App Node 1 (VM)" APISIX_DC1["APISIX Gateway"] CMS_DC1[CMS Microservice] OPA_DC1[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP_DC1[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 1 (VM)" ETCD_DC1[(etcd)] VECTOR_DB_DC1[(Vector DB)] end end subgraph "Datacenter 2 (Active)" subgraph "App Node 2 (VM)" APISIX_DC2["APISIX Gateway"] CMS_DC2[CMS Microservice] OPA_DC2[OPA Microservice] RAG_APP_DC2[RAG Application Service] end subgraph "Data Node 2 (VM)" ETCD_DC2[(etcd)] VECTOR_DB_DC2[(Vector DB)] end end subgraph "External & Managed Services" LLM["Large Language Models"] MANAGED_MONGO["MongoDB Atlas
(Platform Data)"] COSMOS_DB["Azure Cosmos DB
(RAG App Data)"] MANAGED_VAULT["Managed Vault Service
(e.g., HCP Vault)"] end %% --- Connections --- PLATFORM_ADMIN --> LB PROJECT_USER --> LB LB --> APISIX_DC1 & APISIX_DC2 %% Gateway to Backend Services APISIX_DC1 --> CMS_DC1; APISIX_DC1 --> OPA_DC1; APISIX_DC1 --> RAG_APP_DC1 APISIX_DC2 --> CMS_DC2; APISIX_DC2 --> OPA_DC2; APISIX_DC2 --> RAG_APP_DC2 %% Backend Services to Self-Hosted Data Stores CMS_DC1 & CMS_DC2 --> ETCD_DC1 & ETCD_DC2 OPA_DC1 & OPA_DC2 --> ETCD_DC1 & ETCD_DC2 RAG_APP_DC1 & RAG_APP_DC2 --> VECTOR_DB_DC1 & VECTOR_DB_DC2 %% Secure Connections to Managed Services APISIX_DC1 & APISIX_DC2 -- "Secure Connection" --> MANAGED_VAULT CMS_DC1 & CMS_DC2 -- "Secure Connection" --> MANAGED_MONGO RAG_APP_DC1 & RAG_APP_DC2 -- "Secure Connection" --> COSMOS_DB %% Hairpin and External LLM RAG_APP_DC1 & RAG_APP_DC2 --> APISIX_DC1 & APISIX_DC2 APISIX_DC1 & APISIX_DC2 -.->|Proxies to| LLM %% Data Replication for Self-Hosted Components ETCD_DC1 <-->|Replication| ETCD_DC2 VECTOR_DB_DC1 <-->|Replication| VECTOR_DB_DC2

ai.summary: A production-grade, active-active architecture across two datacenters, featuring OPA for policy, etcd for configuration, and a RAG application, integrating
ai.whatItShows: This diagram illustrates a highly available, active-active system deployed across two datacenters. It showcases end-user interaction through a Load Balancer to APISIX Gateways, routing to CMS, OPA, and RAG microservices. Configuration and policy data are managed by etcd, while RAG application data uses a Vector DB. The architecture integrates external managed services like MongoDB Atlas, Azure Cosmos DB, Managed Vault, and Large Language Models, emphasizing secure connections and data replication between datacenters.
ai.whenToUse: Use this architecture when designing a highly available, geo-distributed system that requires robust configuration management, policy enforcement (OPA), and integrates Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) applications with both self-hosted and managed cloud services. Ideal for critical enterprise applications needing resilience and scalability.
ai.howToAdapt: This architecture can be adapted by replacing specific components like APISIX with other API Gateways (e.g., Nginx, Envoy), etcd with Consul or ZooKeeper for distributed key-value store, or OPA with other policy engines. Managed services can be swapped for alternatives from different cloud providers (e.g., AWS DocumentDB instead of MongoDB Atlas, GCP Firestore instead of Cosmos DB). Scaling can be achieved by adding more App Nodes and Data Nodes within each datacenter.
